intro: Government and telecommunications entities were the primary targets of surveillance-related cyber activity in mid-April 2026, with notable impact in the United States and Iran. The period saw five reports, including a critical vulnerability, CVE-2026-22898, which carried a CVSS score of 8.8. The activity spanned multiple sectors, including civil-society, private-sector, and technology, with a mix of severities ranging from informational to critical. This collection of reporting provides insight into the technical vulnerabilities and sectoral targeting that characterized this wave of surveillance.
